title: Electrical, ESD, Battery, Fire, Lifting, and Workspace Safety · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic electrical, esd, battery, fire, lifting, and workspace safety ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in safe work authorization and hazard-control plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls.
Match grounding, antistatic bags, mats, humidity, handling, and packaging controls to static-sensitive work.
Recognize energized, capacitor, liquid, damaged-battery, odor, heat, and deformation stop conditions and route them to qualified handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Hazard Survey
steps.instruction:
debug case — A repair bench combines a swollen battery, energized supply, heavy printer, loose jewelry, blocked exit, poor ventilation, and no disposal authority. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls.

Inspect the Hazard Survey trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Hazard Survey.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Hazard Survey, keep Hazard Survey connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Hazard Survey, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

stimulus.title: Hazard Survey case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A repair bench combines a swollen battery, energized supply, heavy printer, loose jewelry, blocked exit, poor ventilation, and no disposal authority. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: ESD is the only meaningful hazard at a computer repair bench.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls. Artifact under review: safe work authorization and hazard-control plan.

options.text: The Hazard Survey record says safe work authorization and hazard-control plan looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Hazard Survey.

For inspect the failure record, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Hazard Survey for safe work authorization and hazard-control plan.
The record names the assumption, applies Hazard Survey, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Environmental Disposal
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Environmental Disposal: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A repair bench combines a swollen battery, energized supply, heavy printer, loose jewelry, blocked exit, poor ventilation, and no disposal authority. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate reuse, data sanitization, battery handling, hazardous waste, recycling, and jurisdiction-specific documentation.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Environmental Disposal.
steps.why: Retrieving Environmental Disposal through the concrete safe work authorization and hazard-control plan case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Environmental Disposal, keep Environmental Disposal connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate reuse, data sanitization, battery handling, hazardous waste, recycling, and jurisdiction-specific documentation.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Environmental Disposal, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Environmental Disposal precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Environmental Disposal, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A repair bench combines a swollen battery, energized supply, heavy printer, loose jewelry, blocked exit, poor ventilation, and no disposal authority. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate reuse, data sanitization, battery handling, hazardous waste, recycling, and jurisdiction-specific documentation.

For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Environmental Disposal, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Deleting files makes any electronic device safe to donate or recycle.
